chunk_summary: Guidance on resource-tiered feeding pathways for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants when human milk fortifier (HMF) is unavailable, emphasizing safe alternatives, explicit 'do-not' instructions, and structured supplementation plans.
learning_objectives: Understand resource-tiered feeding strategies for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants post-discharge when HMF is unavailable.
Identify safe alternatives to HMF, including nutrient-enriched formula and preterm formula powder as fortifiers.
Recognize unsafe feeding practices and implement explicit 'do-not' instructions in discharge plans.
Learn the importance of follow-up, feeding logs, and supervised feeding interventions in low-resource settings.
key_facts: Human milk fortifier (HMF) access and safe handling are variable in India, requiring alternative feeding pathways.
Structured fortifier workflows should include explicit weaning criteria and written mixing plans for safety.
Post-discharge formula or preterm formula powder can be used as fortifiers under supervision when HMF is unavailable.
Unsafe practices include adding sugar, honey, ghee, cereal water, or protein powders to milk feeds.
Reconstituted formula should not be stored at room temperature for prolonged periods or reused after a feed.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: Randomized trial evidence and WHO evidence summaries include using preterm formula powder as a fortifier for expressed human milk in some settings, but it must be paired with a written mixing plan because composition differs by product.
document_key_topics: Safe alternatives to human milk fortifier
